title: Layered Undo with Restore, Reset, Revert, and References · Git Stakeholder Mission
summary: A public-interest software incident team. The team needs a nondestructive object and reflog investigation, retained recovery refs, layer-correct undo, changed-behavior validation, and an accessible evidence timeline. Planned artifact: Deliver Repository Recovery and Forensics Runbook with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation.
Distinguish reachable, unreachable, dangling, promised, missing, and corrupt objects using fsck and reference evidence.
Create a protective ref, inspect recovered tree and history, compare intended content, and validate changed behavior before integration.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
project case — Case G45 is a project in a disposable repository. only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available. The learner must predict HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction, run git reset --mixed HEAD^, explain the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable, test this changed condition: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for capture repository boundaries, object format, refs, worktrees, index state, operation state, and recovery anchors before investigation.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-undo-recovery-verification-gpxovq" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Undo Recovery Verification into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-undo-recovery-verification-gpxovq
# scenario-key: trace-gpxovq probe-qvoxpg boundary-xovqgp owner-gpxovqx fault-gpxovqz
# before-ref: main=a11cegp candidate=b00b1vq
# hypothesis: only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available
git reset --mixed HEAD^
# expected: HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction
# observation: the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable
# changed-case: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history
# after-ref: main=a11cegp candidate=c0ffeeq
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity
# transfer-limit: preview untracked and ignored data separately before any real hard reset or clean operation
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery
steps.instruction: Write the project evidence note for Gita Undo Recovery Verification: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G53 is a project in a disposable repository. only the local tip ref and index must move while the working content stays available. The learner must predict HEAD moves one parent, the index resets, and working-tree changes remain for reconstruction, run git reset --mixed HEAD^, explain the local commit disappeared from main while its paths remain unstaged and recoverable, test this changed condition: treat the same commit as published and choose revert instead of moving shared history, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain refs/recovery/pre-undo at the original tip and verify reflog plus tree identity.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve refs or patches, preview affected paths and commits, perform the smallest repair, and verify topology, content, behavior, and remote impact.
